DDR5 MEMORY PRICES UP NEARLY 10X

DDR4 is older memory that was being phased out but it's spiking too as $MU, Samsung & SK Hynix shift wafer capacity from conventional memory into HBM for AI chips.

The AI cycle is pulling on compute, memory, storage & servers at the same time with pricing power now flowing through the rest of the hardware stack.

My wife walks into my office and tells me the $RKLB position I put in her IRA is doing 'really well.' 

I opened the account and it went from ~$5K to over $116K.

She delivered the most understated 20-bagger update I’ve ever heard.

Great to be featured in the Wall Street Journal today talking about the $CBRS IPO.

Cerebras is attacking one of the biggest bottlenecks in the AI economy like running massive models faster, cheaper and more efficiently as the world shifts from training to inference deployment.

But the difference between a great company and a great stock is entry price. At a ~$70B valuation, Cerebras is already being priced like a major AI infrastructure winner before I've even seen multiple quarters of execution.

$TSLA CEO Elon Musk and $NVDA CEO Jensen Huang were spotted together on Air Force One.

Two of the biggest builders behind the AI and space economy with a combined net worth north of $1T.
Markets chose violence to close the week.

Today is the end of an era with Jerome Powell’s final day as Fed Chair.

Fun fact: a $10K investment in $NVDA when when his first term began would now be worth over $436K.

$NVDA AI chip business is more than 400x larger than $CBRS and still growing nearly as fast.

Meanwhile, Cerebras is trading around 134x trailing revenue which is more than 5x Nvidia’s multiple.

Jensen Huang stopped by a local noodle shop during his China trip.

This doesn't look like someone worried about disappointing on $NVDA earnings next week.

He says Pershing Square started building a $MSFT position in February and has now made it a core holding.

He says Microsoft still “offers compelling value.”

In today's  Early Look: "The Future of Wall Street Is Already Here"

Bohm wrote that in 1980. He wasn't talking about AI. He was talking about the nature of reality itself — that nothing is a fixed object, everything is a process becoming something else.

Forty-five years later, I spent three days at HedgeyeLIVE watching that sentence come to life in real time.

The future of Wall Street isn't coming. It's already here. 

You can see it in how our power users are working, how signals are being processed, how decisions are being made.

AI DATA CENTER LOAD IS PUSHING UP PJM POWER COSTS

PJM is the largest U.S. grid, serving 67M people across 13 states and D.C.

Wholesale power costs on PJM averaged $136.53/MWh in Q1 2026.

That is up 75.5% from $77.78/MWh in Q1 2025.

Capacity costs jumped 398.1% YoY.

Congestion costs rose 300.4% to $2.02B.

Monitoring Analytics says recent PJM capacity auctions were not competitive, primarily because of forecast demand from data centers.

Bloomberg says data center load included in the last two PJM capacity auctions increased customer bills by $13.8B.

The robots have clocked in.

@JAL_Official_jp is deploying humanoids at Haneda Airport as part of a three-year operational commitment.

@BMW validated humanoid robots through over 1,250 operational hours on an active assembly line and is scaling to Europe.

$AMZN is building a consumer physical AI platform.

@UnitreeRobotics is targeting an IPO valuation of USD $3-7 billion.

This is no longer a science project.

The supply chain question is where the real investment thesis is being built, and the EV analogy from 2012 is instructive: the most durable value will likely accrue to the component categories with the highest technical barriers, actuators, force sensors, and precision reducers, not the headline names.

$KOID holds 50 companies across the broad humanoid and physical AI ecosystem, equal-weighted and globally diversified across the US, China, Japan, and Europe.

Similar to what $NVDA and semiconductors did for digital AI, these are the picks and shovels for physical AI.
